title: malva × intermedia
intro: Malva × intermedia, also known as marshmallow or common mallow, is a hybrid plant species that belongs to the mallow family and is characterized by its large, rounded leaves and showy pink or white flowers. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory, soothing, and antimicrobial effects, which can help to calm irritated skin and mucous membranes. Malva × intermedia has several health benefits, including aiding in the treatment of digestive issues, respiratory problems, and skin conditions such as eczema and acne, due to its high mucilage content. The plant can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or syrup, and is also used in topical creams and ointments to provide relief from irritation and inflammation.
